ccel.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

source.title: ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second
ANF04. Fathers of the Third Century: Tertullian, Part Fourth; Minucius Felix; Commodian; Origen, Parts First and Second

verses.text: And Saturn the old, if he is a god, how does he grow old?
Or if he was a god, why was he driven by his terrors to devour his children?
But because he was not a god, he consumed the bowels of his sons in a monstrous madness.
He was a king upon earth, born in the mount Olympus; and he was not divine, but called himself a god.
He fell into weakness of mind, and swallowed a stone for his son.
Thus he became a god; of late he is called Jupiter.
